pi_items.source: Web search for CIA World Factbook confirmed URL exists and is accessible
Web search confirmed /wanted is a valid FBI directory path with multiple subpages including /wanted/fugitives, /wanted/topten, /wanted/cyber, /wanted/wanted_terrorists, /wanted/seeking-info
Web search for OFAC confirmed this is the official OFAC sanctions programs page
Web search shows OFAC's official domain is ofac.treasury.gov, not the parent domain treasury.gov
10240 bytes equals 10 KB, a reasonable minimum for a complete website archive. No contradictory information found.

pi_items.verified_value: Real US government websites with actual public data
pi_items.source: All three target URLs point to legitimate, publicly accessible US government websites (CIA, FBI, Treasury OFAC). The task involves archiving real public data, not synthetic data.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves archiving three real US government websites: the CIA World Factbook Countries page (https://www.cia.gov/the-world-factbook/countries/), the FBI Wanted page (https://www.fbi.gov/wanted), and OFAC Sanctions Programs page (https://ofac.treasury.gov/sanctions-programs-and-country-information). All three URLs are legitimate and publicly accessible government resources. The data being archived is real public information, not synthetic. The only metadata discrepancy found is that the OFAC target domain is listed as 'treasury.gov' in the metadata, but the actual authoritative domain is 'ofac.treasury.gov'. The minimum file size threshold of 10,240 bytes (10 KB) is appropriate for complete website archives.
